title: Oracle Connector Snapshot and Stream Transition
interaction_flow: OracleSnapshotChangeEventSource.readTableStructure() → jdbcConnection reads DDL for captured tables → OracleDatabaseSchema updated
OracleSnapshotChangeEventSource → OracleConnection: flashback query SELECT * FROM table AS OF SCN <snapshotScn>
OracleSnapshotChangeEventSource → EventDispatcher: dispatchDataChangeEvent(partition, tableId, offset, READ) [for each row]
OracleSnapshotChangeEventSource → OracleOffsetContext: snapshotScn is persisted in offset map as SNAPSHOT_SCN_KEY
→ this.effectiveOffset = offsetContext (contains snapshotScn from snapshot phase)
[if offsetScn == snapshotScn — first run after snapshot]:
→ if pending TXs: find min startScn among pending transactions (those straddling snapshot boundary)
[fail-fast]: if offsetScn < firstScn - 1 → throw DebeziumException('Please perform a new snapshot')
AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource → LogFileCollector: collect log files for [startScn, endScn]
→ reads: SCN, SQL_REDO, OPERATION_CODE, TX_ID, TABLE_NAME, ROW_ID, ROLLBACK_FLAG, RS_ID, STATUS, START_SCN, COMMIT_SCN, SEQUENCE, ...
→ getSqlRedo() [handles CSF=1 multi-row continuation, up to 100KB / 25 rows]
SELECT_LOB_LOCATOR / LOB_WRITE / LOB_ERASE / XML_BEGIN / XML_WRITE / XML_END → specialized handlers
key_relationships: AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource depends_on OracleConnectorConfig (strategy, batch size, sleep time, archive retention)
OracleOffsetContext holds: Scn scn, Scn snapshotScn, Map<String,Scn> snapshotPendingTransactions, CommitScn commitScn, boolean snapshotCompleted
OracleConnectorConfig.SnapshotMode enum: ALWAYS, INITIAL (default), INITIAL_ONLY, NO_DATA, RECOVERY

scn_watermark_logic.snapshot_scn_capture: Captured at determineSnapshotOffset() via adapter, stored in OracleOffsetContext.snapshotScn and persisted as SNAPSHOT_SCN_KEY in offset map
scn_watermark_logic.first_mining_session_detection: offsetScn == snapshotScn signals first streaming run post-snapshot
scn_watermark_logic.pending_tx_boundary: computeStartScnForFirstMiningSession() walks snapshotPendingTransactions to find min startScn; if older than archive log availability, warns about data loss
scn_watermark_logic.commit_scn_guard: commitScn forced to snapshotScn-1 to prevent double-emitting pre-snapshot committed transactions
scn_watermark_logic.fail_fast: If offsetScn < firstScn-1 (not continuous mining), throws DebeziumException and demands a new snapshot
scn_watermark_logic.archive_log_only: isArchiveLogOnlyModeAndScnIsNotAvailable() check can cause early return if SCN not yet in archive logs

it_test_observations: shouldContinueWithStreamingAfterSnapshot(): INSERT 2 rows → start connector → consume snapshot records → INSERT 1 more → consume streaming record
Snapshot first record: sourceOffset[snapshot]=INITIAL, snapshot_completed=false, source[snapshot]='first'
Snapshot last record: sourceOffset[snapshot]=INITIAL, snapshot_completed=true, source[snapshot]='last'
SnapshotMode.NO_DATA skips table data snapshot but still streams changes
TransactionSnapshotBoundaryMode.SKIP allows bypassing archive log check for INITIAL_ONLY
important_notes: The snapshot-to-stream handoff is driven entirely by SCN comparison: when offsetScn==snapshotScn, streaming knows it's starting fresh. Pending transactions straddling the snapshot boundary are tracked in the offset map so mining starts before the snapshot SCN to capture those TXs. The SQL REDO continuation (CSF flag) allows up to 100KB of LOB/XML SQL across 25 result-set rows. executeLogMiningStreaming() is abstract — concrete adapters (Buffered/Unbuffered) implement the mining loop and transaction buffering strategy.
